title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet service file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kubelet service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Automated AAC auditing has been modified to allow CIS-CAT to input a variable for the <PATH>/<FILENAME> of the kubelet service config file. Please set $kubelet_service_config=<PATH> based on the file location on your system for example: export kubelet_service_config=/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf To perform the audit manually: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf
input_row.default_value: By default, kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using commands to check the ownership of the kubelet service file, which indicates that it can be verified through standard Kubernetes practices.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of the kubelet service file's ownership for maintaining integrity, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The audit mentions using `stat` to check the file ownership, which implies that the property can be queried through a command that can be automated.
- There is no requirement for SSH/RDP access to instances, as the audit can be performed via Kubernetes commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_service_file_ownership_set_to_root

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl exec <node-name> -- stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf` to check the ownership of the kubelet service file.
- The resource to enumerate is the kubelet service file located at `/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/kubeadm.conf`.
- The expected configuration property is that the ownership should be `root:root`.
- Pass/Fail criteria: 
  - Pass: If the output of the command returns `root:root`.
  - Fail: If the output returns anything other than `root:root`.
